front: Como está o andamento do novo projeto?
pronunciation: KOH-moo ees-TAH oo ahn-dah-MEHN-too doo NOH-voo proo-ZHEH-too?
back: How is the progress of the new project?
context: Asking for a generic status update using 'andamento' (progress/walk of things).

front: Eu já finalizei a primeira parte do relatório.
pronunciation: ew zhah fee-nah-lee-ZAY ah pree-MAY-rah PAHR-chee doo heh-lah-TOH-ryoo.
back: I have already finalized the first part of the report.
context: Reporting task completion using the crisp professional verb 'finalizar'.

front: Precisamos definir as prioridades da semana.
pronunciation: prah-see-ZAH-moos jee-fee-NEER ahs pryo-ree-DAH-jees dah seh-MAH-nah.
back: We need to define the priorities for the week.
context: Aligning team focuses using 'definir prioridades'.

front: Você pode me enviar a planilha por e-mail?
pronunciation: voh-SEH POH-jee mee eehn-vy-AHR ah plah-NEEL-yah poor e-mail?
back: Can you send me the spreadsheet by email?
context: Requesting an asset using the standard office noun 'planilha' (spreadsheet).

front: A reunião foi adiada para as duas da tarde.
pronunciation: ah hehoo-nee-OHNG foy ah-jee-AH-dah PAH-rah ahs DOO-ahs dah TAHR-jee.
back: The meeting was postponed to two in the afternoon.
context: Communicating a schedule shift using the passive structure 'foi adiada'.

front: Estou com dificuldades para rodar o script.
pronunciation: ees-TOY koong jee-fee-kool-DAH-jees PAH-rah hoo-DAHR oo script.
back: I am having difficulties running the script.
context: Flagging a technical workflow blocker using 'rodar' (to run/execute software).

front: Amanhã vou trabalhar em regime de home office.
pronunciation: ah-mah-NYAH vow trah-bah-LYAHR ahyng heh-ZHEE-mee jee home office.
back: Tomorrow I'm going to work from home / remote setup.
context: Using the ubiquitous English loan expression 'home office' to describe remote working.

front: Quem vai ficar responsável pela ata da reunião?
pronunciation: kayng vy fee-KAHR hehs-pohn-SAH-vew PEH-lah AH-tah dah hehoo-nee-OHNG?
back: Who is going to be responsible for the meeting minutes?
context: Delegating administrative tracking documentation using the noun 'ata' (minutes).

front: Temos que entregar o escopo até sexta-feira.
pronunciation: TEH-moos kee eehn-treh-GAHR oo ees-KOH-poo ah-TEH SEHKS-tah FAY-rah.
back: We have to deliver the scope by Friday.
context: Stating project constraints using the structural technical noun 'escopo'.

front: Vou agendar uma chamada de alinhamento com a equipe.
pronunciation: vow ah-zhehn-DAHR OO-mah shah-MAH-dah jee ah-leeng-yah-MEHN-too koong ah ee-KEE-pee.
back: I will schedule an alignment call with the team.
context: Using 'chamada de alinhamento' to refer to a sync or alignment meeting.

front: Houve um mal-entendido sobre as metas do trimestre.
pronunciation: OH-vee oong maw-ehyn-tehn-DEE-doo SOH-bree ahs MEH-tahs doo tree-MEHS-tree.
back: There was a misunderstanding about the quarter's goals.
context: Using the compound noun 'mal-entendido' to softly unpack a team communication issue.

front: Vou precisar fazer algumas horas extras hoje.
pronunciation: vow prah-see-ZAHR fah-ZEHR aw-GOO-mahs OH-rahs EKS-trahs OH-zhee.
back: I'm going to need to do some overtime today.
context: Stating an extended work schedule layout using 'horas extras'.

front: Você pode revisar essa linha de código para mim?
pronunciation: voh-SEH POH-jee heh-vee-ZAHR EHS-ah LEENG-yah jee KOH-jee-goo PAH-rah meeyng?
back: Can you review this line of code for me?
context: Asking a peer for code review validation using 'linha de código'.

front: O feedback do gerente foi muito positivo.
pronunciation: oo feedback doo zheh-REHN-chee foy MOOI-too poo-zee-VEE-voo.
back: The manager's feedback was very positive.
context: Referencing performance evaluations using the universally accepted loanword 'feedback'.

front: Estamos sem orçamento para novas contratações.
pronunciation: ees-TAH-moos sayng oohr-sah-MEHN-too PAH-rah NOH-vahs kohn-trah-tah-SOHNGS.
back: We are without budget for new hirings.
context: Stating corporate resource constraints using 'orçamento' (budget).

front: Eu costumo fazer uma pausa rápida para o café.
pronunciation: ew koos-TOO-moo fah-ZEHR OO-mah POW-zah HAH-pee-dah PAH-rah oo kah-FEH.
back: I usually take a quick coffee break.
context: Describing regular daily habits in an office layout using 'fazer uma pausa'.

front: O prazo final para o deploy é hoje à meia-noite.
pronunciation: oo PRAH-zoo fee-NAW PAH-rah oo deploy eh OH-zhee ah MAY-ah NOY-chee.
back: The final deadline for deployment is tonight at midnight.
context: Using technical jargon 'deploy' inside corporate timing parameters.

front: Por favor, me avise quando o servidor estiver pronto.
pronunciation: poor fah-VOHR, mee ah-VEE-zee KWAHN-doo oo sehr-vee-DOHR ees-chee-VEHR PROHN-too.
back: Please let me know when the server is ready.
context: The temporal element 'quando' triggers the future subjunctive 'estiver'.

front: Temos uma demanda muito alta nesta época do ano.
pronunciation: TEH-moos OO-mah jee-MAHN-dah MOOI-too AW-tah NEHS-tah EH-poo-kah doo AH-noo.
back: We have a very high demand at this time of the year.
context: Explaining seasonal workflow stress using 'demanda alta'.

front: Vou compartilhar a minha tela durante a apresentação.
pronunciation: vow kohm-pahr-chee-LYAHR ah MEENG-yah TEH-lah doo-RAHN-chee ah ah-preh-zehn-tah-SOHNG.
back: I will share my screen during the presentation.
context: A phrase used daily in video calls: 'compartilhar a tela'.

front: O documento está salvo na pasta compartilhada.
pronunciation: oo doo-koo-MEHN-too ees-TAH SAW-voo nah PAHS-tah kohm-pahr-chee-LYA...-dah.
back: The document is saved in the shared folder.
context: Directing a team member to a directory asset using 'pasta' (folder).

front: Quem é o responsável por aprovar os gastos?
pronunciation: kayng eh oo hehs-pohn-SAH-vew poor ah-proo-VAHR ooz GAHS-toos?
back: Who is responsible for approving the expenses?
context: Querying budget signing authority or administrative workflow loops.

front: Precisamos documentar o passo a passo da tarefa.
pronunciation: prah-see-ZAH-moos doo-koo-mehn-TAHR oo PAHS-oo ah PAHS-oo dah tah-REH-fah.
back: We need to document the step-by-step of the task.
context: Using the idiomatic nominal phrase 'passo a passo' for thorough technical instructions.

front: Vou enviar um resumo dos pontos discutidos.
pronunciation: vow eehn-vy-AHR oong heh-ZOO-moo doos POHN-toos dees-koo-CHEE-doos.
back: I will send a summary of the discussed points.
context: Following up post-meeting using 'resumo' (summary/wrapup).

front: Nosso principal objetivo é otimizar o desempenho.
pronunciation: NOH-soo preeng-see-PAW ohb-zheh-CHEE-voo eh oo-chee-mee-ZAHR oo dehs-eeymp-AY-nyoo.
back: Our main objective is to optimize performance.
context: Stating technical core goals using the abstract corporate noun 'desempenho' (performance).

front: Essa nova funcionalidade vai agregar muito valor.
pronunciation: EHS-ah NOH-vah foon-syo-nah-lee-DAH-jee vy ah-greh-GAHR MOOI-too vah-LOHR.
back: This new feature is going to add a lot of value.
context: Pitching a feature enhancement or update using 'funcionalidade' (feature/functionality).

front: Ainda estou me adaptando à cultura da empresa.
pronunciation: ah-EEYN-dah ees-TOY mee ah-dahp-TAHN-doo ah kool-TOO-rah dah eeym-PREH-zah.
back: I am still adapting to the company's culture.
context: Expressing professional transition status using the gerund 'adaptando'.

front: Houve uma queda de energia no escritório.
pronunciation: OH-vee OO-mah KEH-dah jee eh-nehr-ZHEE-ah noo ees-kree-TOH-ryoo.
back: There was a power outage at the office.
context: Reporting physical office workspace disruption using 'queda de energia'.

front: Podemos marcar um bate-papo rápido depois?
pronunciation: poo-DEH-moos mahr-KAHR oong BAH-chee PAHP-oo HAH-pee-doo jee-POYS?
back: Can we schedule a quick chat later?
context: Using the casual noun 'bate-papo' (informal chat/catch up) within a working environment.

front: O sistema está instável desde cedo.
pronunciation: oo sees-TEH-mah ees-TAH eeyns-TAH-vew DEHS-jee SEE-doo.
back: The system has been unstable since early morning.
context: Reporting infrastructural runtime defects using 'instável'.

front: Você tem dez minutos para conversar?
pronunciation: voh-SEH cheyng dehs mee-NOO-toos PAH-rah kohn-vehr-SAHR?
back: Do you have ten minutes to talk?
context: A classic baseline approach query before initiating ad-hoc discussions.

front: Ficou decidido que mudaremos a abordagem.
pronunciation: fee-KOY jee-see-JEE-doo kee moo-dah-REH-moos ah ah-boohr-DAH-zhehng.
back: It was decided that we will change the approach.
context: Summarizing an executive choice using 'abordagem' (approach/methodology).

front: Esta tarefa é de baixa complexidade.
pronunciation: EHS-tah tah-REH-fah eh jee BUY-shah kohm-plehk-see-DAH-jee.
back: This task is of low complexity.
context: Assessing sprint tickets or work estimation metrics.

front: Caso precise de algo, estou à disposição.
pronunciation: KAH-zoo prah-SEE-zee jee AWL-goo, ees-TOY ah dees-poo-zee-SOHNG.
back: In case you need anything, I am at your disposal.
context: A professional courtesy sign-off phrase triggering subjunctive 'precise'.

front: Bom trabalho a todos, nos vemos na segunda-feira.
pronunciation: boong trah-BAH-lyoo ah TOH-doos, noos VEH-moos nah seh-GOON-dah FAY-rah.
back: Good work everyone, see you on Monday.
context: A warm weekend wrap-up statement to wrap up the professional sequence.
